pattern: Each subtree only needs to report how far its own leaves sit from it; merging two children lets leaves from different sides pair up whenever their combined depth stays within the limit.
hints: Think post-order: what does a subtree need to hand back to its parent about the leaves inside it — not the leaves themselves, but how far each one currently sits from this node.

At a node, gather the list of leaf depths coming from the left child and from the right child (a leaf itself contributes a single depth of zero), then count every left-right pair whose depths sum to at most the limit.
Return upward one combined list of those depths incremented by one, dropping any that already exceed the limit, so higher ancestors can keep pairing across further leaves.

edge_inputs.description: two leaves directly under the root
distance one step too small excludes the only pair
a full small tree pairs only adjacent leaf siblings
a long skewed chain has at most one leaf
unbalanced tree mixing shallow and deep leaves

stress.description: random binary tree with a modest node budget
